entries.purpose: Shows the TX DSP signal flow as a horizontal strip of stages (EQ, Compressor, Gate, De-Ess, Tube, PUDU, Reverb). Click a stage to bypass it, double-click to open its editor, drag to reorder the chain. A single BYPASS button disables every stage at once. Also hosts the post-PUDU monitor recorder.
entries.how_to_open: Always visible as the top section of the 'PooDoo Audio' (TXDSP) container when that tile is enabled. Toggle the container via the applet-tray button labelled 'PUDU' in the right sidebar.

controls.label: TX
controls.behavior: Shows and edits the TX DSP chain (the interactive, fully-implemented mode).
controls.notes: Part of an exclusive pair with RX; amber 'PooDoo' colour when selected.

controls.label: RX
controls.behavior: Switches to the RX chain view — currently shows the placeholder 'Client RX chain — coming in a future phase'.
controls.notes: Placeholder only; BYPASS is a no-op in this mode.

controls.label: BYPASS
controls.behavior: Checked: snapshots the currently-enabled stages and disables all of them. Unchecked: re-enables just the stages that were on before.
controls.notes: Stages toggled manually while BYPASS was active are preserved outside the snapshot.

controls.label: Record (circle glyph)
controls.behavior: Captures up to 30 s of post-PUDU TX audio; click again to stop (playback starts automatically).
controls.notes: Tooltip: 'Record up to 30 s of post-PooDoo TX audio (MIC must be set to PC and DAX off).' Only enabled when the mic input is ready and playback is not running. Pulses red while recording.

controls.label: Play (triangle glyph)
controls.behavior: Plays back the captured PUDU audio; click again to cancel.
controls.notes: Only enabled once a recording exists and recording is not active. Pulses green while playing.

controls.label: Chain stage (Eq)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the EQ stage; double-click opens the EQ editor; drag reorders the chain.
controls.notes: Delegated to ClientChainWidget; hint text below reads 'Click to bypass · Double click to edit · Drag to reorder'.

controls.label: Chain stage (Comp)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the compressor; double-click opens the compressor editor; drag reorders.

controls.label: Chain stage (Gate)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the gate; double-click opens the gate editor; drag reorders.

controls.label: Chain stage (DeEss)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the de-esser; double-click opens the de-ess editor; drag reorders.

controls.label: Chain stage (Tube)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the tube saturator; double-click opens the tube editor; drag reorders.

controls.label: Chain stage (Enh / PUDU)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the PUDU exciter; double-click opens the PUDU editor; drag reorders.

controls.label: Chain stage (Reverb)
controls.behavior: Single-click toggles bypass for the reverb; double-click opens the reverb editor; drag reorders.

indicators.label: TX endpoint indicator
indicators.meaning: Red pulse while the user is transmitting on their own slice (driven by TransmitModel::moxChanged).

indicators.label: MIC ready indicator
indicators.meaning: Enables / disables the record button based on mic source (PC) and DAX state.

indicators.label: Monitor record pulse
indicators.meaning: 500 ms pulse while the post-PUDU recorder is capturing audio.

indicators.label: Monitor play pulse
indicators.meaning: 500 ms pulse while captured audio is being played back.

indicators.label: Interaction hint
states: Click to bypass · Double click to edit · Drag to reorder
indicators.meaning: Static usage hint, shown only in TX mode.

related_menu_items: Right-click the applet tray button 'PUDU' > toggle PooDoo Audio container visibility
related_user_tasks: Reorder the TX DSP chain
Bypass every TX stage at once
Re-enable a specific stage after a global bypass
Open a stage's floating editor from the chain
Record up to 30 seconds of post-PUDU TX audio
Play back the captured PUDU audio
Switch between TX chain view and RX placeholder
Visually confirm when TX (MOX) is live

entries.purpose: TX-side dynamic-range compressor. Shows the static transfer curve with a live 'ball' riding on the current envelope, a horizontal gain-reduction meter (20 dB max), and five tuning knobs so you can tame voice peaks without opening the floating editor.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'COMPRESSOR' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Tile stays hidden until the Compressor stage is enabled (bypass off) via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: Transfer curve
controls.behavior: Compact-mode ClientCompCurveWidget. Draws the input/output transfer curve plus a live ball showing the current envelope level.
controls.notes: View-only in the applet; editable in the floating ClientCompEditor.

controls.label: Gain-reduction bar
controls.behavior: Horizontal amber strip, right-filled. Scale maxes at 20 dB reduction; a tick at -6 dB marks a typical working amount.
controls.notes: Refreshed ~30 Hz from ClientComp::gainReductionDb() with MeterSmoother ballistics.

controls.label: Thresh
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Sets the level above which compression starts.
controls.notes: Label formatted as '-18.0 dB'.

controls.label: Ratio
controls.behavior: Logarithmic mapping (1 * 20^n). Sets how hard peaks are held once threshold is crossed.
controls.notes: Label formatted as 'X.XX:1'.

controls.label: Attack
controls.behavior: Exponential mapping (0.1 * 3000^n). Sets how quickly the compressor clamps down after the threshold is crossed.
controls.notes: Label formatted 'X.X ms' below 10, 'X ms' above.

controls.label: Release
controls.behavior: Exponential mapping (5 * 400^n). Sets how quickly gain returns after the input drops back below threshold.
controls.notes: Label formatted 'X ms'.

controls.label: Makeup
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Adds back gain lost to compression.
controls.notes: Label shows explicit '+' sign for positive values.

indicators.label: Envelope ball
indicators.meaning: Live input level plotted against the static transfer curve.

indicators.label: Gain-reduction strip
indicators.meaning: Amount of dynamic attenuation currently applied by the compressor.

related_menu_items: Double-click the Comp stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating Compressor editor
Right-click the 'COMPRESSOR' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Adjust compressor threshold
Set compression ratio for voice
Tune attack / release for a natural-sounding squeeze
Apply make-up gain after compression
Watch live gain reduction while speaking
Bypass the compressor from the chain
Open the full Compressor editor for knee and limiter controls

entries.purpose: TX-side de-esser. Tames harsh 'S' and 'T' sibilance by ducking a narrow band when it exceeds a sidechain threshold. Shows the sidechain bandpass response, a 24 dB gain-reduction meter, and four tuning knobs.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'DESS' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Hidden until the De-Ess stage is enabled via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: Sidechain response curve
controls.behavior: Compact-mode ClientDeEssCurveWidget. Draws the bandpass filter response with a live ball at the current centre frequency.

controls.label: Gain-reduction bar
controls.behavior: Horizontal soft-red strip, right-filled. Scale maxes at 24 dB; a tick marks the -6 dB typical amount.
controls.notes: Refreshed ~30 Hz from ClientDeEss::gainReductionDb().

controls.label: Freq
controls.behavior: Logarithmic mapping (1000 * 12^n). Sets the centre frequency of the sibilance band.
controls.notes: Label '6.0 kHz' above 1 kHz, 'N Hz' below.

controls.label: Q
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Sets the bandwidth of the sibilance band — higher Q = narrower.
controls.notes: Label 'X.XX'.

controls.label: Thresh
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Level above which the de-esser starts attenuating the band.

controls.label: Amount
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Maximum attenuation applied at peak sibilance.
controls.notes: Values are negative (or zero) because they represent reduction.

indicators.label: Centre-frequency ball
indicators.meaning: Marks the currently-tuned sibilance centre frequency on the response curve.

indicators.label: Gain-reduction strip
indicators.meaning: Current attenuation applied to the sibilance band.

related_menu_items: Double-click the DeEss stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating De-Ess editor
Right-click the 'DESS' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Sweep Freq to locate peak sibilance
Narrow or widen the sidechain band with Q
Set threshold just below the loudest 'S' peaks
Dial Amount for the most transparent de-essing
Watch live GR while reading a sibilant phrase
Bypass the de-esser from the chain

entries.purpose: Client-side parametric equalizer for TX and RX paths. Shows a compact live analyzer overlaid with the summed EQ response; use the RX / TX tabs to pick which path you're shaping. Bands are added, removed, and tuned interactively in the separate ClientEqEditor floating window.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'CEQ' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Hidden until the EQ stage is enabled via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: RX
controls.behavior: Selects the receive-path EQ for display; binds the curve widget to ClientEq RX instance.
controls.notes: Part of a mutually-exclusive pair with TX. Starts checked.

controls.label: TX
controls.behavior: Selects the transmit-path EQ for display; binds the curve widget to ClientEq TX instance.

controls.label: Analyzer / curve area
controls.behavior: ClientEqCurveWidget — 110 px tall. Shows grid, summed EQ response for the selected path, and a live FFT analyzer overlay.
controls.notes: View-only; editing happens in the floating ClientEqEditor.

indicators.label: Summed EQ response
indicators.meaning: Cumulative frequency response of all enabled bands for the selected path.

indicators.label: Live analyzer overlay
indicators.meaning: Real-time FFT of audio passing through the selected path.

related_menu_items: Double-click the EQ stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating EQ editor
Right-click the 'CEQ' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Switch between viewing RX and TX EQ
See the live spectrum of the selected path
Bypass the EQ stage from the chain
Open the floating editor to add / remove / tune bands
Verify the summed curve matches your mental target

entries.purpose: TX-side downward expander / noise gate. Silences background noise between words by attenuating audio below a threshold. Shows the static transfer curve with a live input ball, a 40 dB gain-reduction meter, and five tuning knobs.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'GATE' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Hidden until the Gate stage is enabled via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: Transfer curve
controls.behavior: Compact-mode ClientGateCurveWidget. Plots the expander's static transfer curve and a live ball at the current input level.

controls.label: Gain-reduction bar
controls.behavior: Horizontal amber strip, right-filled. Scale maxes at 40 dB (gates can cut very deep); a tick at -15 dB marks the soft-expander default floor.

controls.label: Thresh
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Level below which the gate starts attenuating.

controls.label: Ratio
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Higher ratios give a harder, more gate-like cut; lower ratios act as a soft downward expander.
controls.notes: Label 'X.X:1'.

controls.label: Attack
controls.behavior: Exponential mapping (0.1 * 1000^n). Sets how quickly the gate opens when input rises above threshold.
controls.notes: Label 'X.XX ms' below 10 ms, 'X.X ms' above.

controls.label: Release
controls.behavior: Exponential mapping (5 * 400^n). Sets how quickly the gate closes after input falls.
controls.notes: Label 'X.X ms' below 100, 'X ms' above.

controls.label: Floor
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Maximum attenuation the gate is allowed to apply.

indicators.label: Input ball
indicators.meaning: Shows whether the gate is currently open or closed.

indicators.label: Gain-reduction strip
indicators.meaning: Depth of attenuation while the gate is closed.

related_menu_items: Double-click the Gate stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating Gate editor
Right-click the 'GATE' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Set threshold just above room noise floor
Choose gate vs soft-expander behaviour via ratio
Tune attack / release for natural open/close
Set Floor to avoid unnatural silence between words
Watch live GR while not speaking
Bypass the gate from the chain

entries.purpose: Centrepiece of the PooDoo Audio chain — the PUDU exciter. Aphex-style Aural Exciter + Big Bottom (Even mode) or Behringer-style tanh shaper + bass compressor (Odd mode). Two groups of three knobs: 'Poo' shapes low end, 'Doo' adds presence / air.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'PUDU' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Hidden until the PUDU stage is enabled via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: PooDoo logo
controls.behavior: Animated branded logo that pulses with the wet-signal RMS.
controls.notes: PooDooLogo widget — 40 px minimum height.

controls.label: Even
controls.behavior: Selects Aphex-lineage asymmetric shaping — predominantly even harmonics, warmer, with Big Bottom LF saturation.
controls.notes: Amber PooDoo-colour when checked. Exclusive with 'Odd'.

controls.label: Odd
controls.behavior: Selects Behringer-lineage symmetric tanh shaping — pure odd harmonics, brighter, with a feed-forward bass compressor.
controls.notes: Exclusive with 'Even'.

controls.label: Poo / Drive
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Drives the low-frequency saturator / compressor harder.
controls.notes: Label 'X.X dB'. First knob under the 'Poo' bracket label.

controls.label: Poo / Tune
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Centres the low-frequency focus band.
controls.notes: Label 'X Hz'.

controls.label: Poo / Mix
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Blends the enhanced low band back with the dry signal.
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage.

controls.label: Doo / Tune
controls.behavior: Logarithmic mapping (1000 * 10^n). Centres the high-frequency excitement band.
controls.notes: Label '5.0 kHz' above 1 kHz, 'X Hz' below. First knob under the 'Doo' bracket label.

controls.label: Doo / Air
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Amount of harmonics / 'air' added at the high band.
controls.notes: Backing setter is ClientPudu::setDooHarmonicsDb — the knob label in the UI is 'Air'.

controls.label: Doo / Mix
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Blends the excited highs back with the dry signal.
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage.

indicators.label: PooDoo logo pulse
indicators.meaning: Brightness follows the processed (wet) signal RMS.

indicators.label: Poo group bracket
indicators.meaning: Group label — the three knobs below belong to the low-frequency processor.

indicators.label: Doo group bracket
indicators.meaning: Group label — the three knobs below belong to the high-frequency processor.

related_menu_items: Double-click the PUDU (Enh) stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating PUDU editor
Right-click the 'PUDU' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Pick Aphex (Even) vs Behringer (Odd) character
Dial Poo Drive for LF thickness
Tune Poo to the fundamental of your voice
Blend the Poo enhancement with Mix
Centre Doo on the presence band for your mic
Add air with Doo Harmonics
Blend the Doo excitement with Mix
Bypass PUDU from the chain

entries.purpose: TX-side Freeverb reverb tail. A compact 5-knob row — Size, Decay, Damp, Pre-delay, Mix — adds room or hall ambience to the transmitted voice.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'REVERB' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Hidden until the Reverb stage is enabled via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: Size
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Sets the modelled room size.
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage.

controls.label: Decay
controls.behavior: Exponential mapping (0.3 * (5.0/0.3)^n, ~16.7x). Sets the reverb tail length.
controls.notes: Label 'X.XX s'.

controls.label: Damp
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Higher values damp high frequencies faster in the tail.
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage.

controls.label: Pre
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Pre-delay between the dry signal and the first reflections.
controls.notes: Label 'X ms'.

controls.label: Mix
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Dry / wet balance.
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage.

related_menu_items: Double-click the Reverb stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating Reverb editor
Right-click the 'REVERB' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Set room size for a small or large-hall feel
Tune decay to taste without muddying speech
Reduce the high-end sparkle of the tail with Damp
Offset reflections from the dry signal with Pre
Dial in a subtle Mix — 10-15 % is typical for voice
Bypass reverb from the chain

entries.purpose: TX-side dynamic tube saturator. Adds harmonic richness by shaping the signal through a bias-swept tube model. Shows a transfer curve that bends with Drive / Bias / model, a live input ball, and five tuning knobs.
entries.how_to_open: Sub-container 'TUBE' inside the PooDoo Audio (TXDSP) parent container. Hidden until the Tube stage is enabled via the CHAIN widget or the floating editor.

controls.label: Transfer curve
controls.behavior: Compact-mode ClientTubeCurveWidget. Draws the currently-configured tube transfer curve with a live ball at the input.

controls.label: Drive
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Pushes more signal into the tube stage.
controls.notes: Label 'X.X dB'.

controls.label: Tone
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Negative values darken, positive brighten the saturated signal.
controls.notes: Label 'X.XX'.

controls.label: Bias
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Shifts the operating point on the transfer curve, changing the harmonic mix.
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage.

controls.label: Output
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Post-tube make-up / trim gain.
controls.notes: Label 'X.X dB'.

controls.label: Mix
controls.behavior: Linear mapping. Dry / wet blend (100 % = fully saturated signal).
controls.notes: Label displayed as percentage. Backing setter is ClientTube::setDryWet.

indicators.label: Live input ball
indicators.meaning: Dot moves along the transfer curve at the current input level, visualising the saturation regime.

related_menu_items: Double-click the Tube stage in the CHAIN widget to open the floating Tube editor
Right-click the 'TUBE' sub-container titlebar to float, pop-out, or hide it
related_user_tasks: Dial Drive until the curve starts to bend
Shift Bias to tweak the even / odd harmonic balance
Brighten or darken the saturated signal with Tone
Compensate level changes with Output
Parallel-blend saturation with Mix
Bypass the tube from the chain

entries.purpose: 8-band graphic equalizer applied inside the radio itself via the TCP/IP API — separate from the client-side parametric EQ. Fixed bands at 63, 125, 250, 500 Hz and 1, 2, 4, 8 kHz; each slider trims +/-10 dB. RX / TX selector switches which path is being shaped.
entries.how_to_open: Right sidebar applet tray button 'EQ' toggles the Equalizer tile. The tile sits in the top row of the applet panel.

controls.label: ON
controls.behavior: Enables or disables the equalizer for the currently-selected path (RX or TX).
controls.notes: Green highlight when enabled. Acts on EqualizerModel::setTxEnabled / setRxEnabled depending on view.

controls.label: Reset arc (revert icon)
controls.behavior: Resets all 8 bands of the currently-selected path back to 0 dB.
controls.notes: Tooltip: 'Reset all bands to 0 dB'. Drawn as a 3/4-circle arrow.

controls.label: RX
controls.behavior: Selects the receive equalizer bands for display / editing.
controls.notes: Part of an exclusive pair with TX. Blue highlight when active.

controls.label: TX
controls.behavior: Selects the transmit equalizer bands for display / editing.
controls.notes: Starts checked — applet opens on the TX view.

controls.behavior: Trims the 63 Hz band for the selected path; value label below slider updates live.
controls.notes: Vertical slider, 100 px tall, no tick marks.

controls.behavior: Trims the 125 Hz band for the selected path.
Trims the 250 Hz band for the selected path.
Trims the 500 Hz band for the selected path.

controls.label: 1k
controls.behavior: Trims the 1 kHz band for the selected path.

controls.label: 2k
controls.behavior: Trims the 2 kHz band for the selected path.

controls.label: 4k
controls.behavior: Trims the 4 kHz band for the selected path.

controls.label: 8k
controls.behavior: Trims the 8 kHz band for the selected path.

controls.label: +10 / 0 / -10 dB scale
controls.behavior: Left and right reference labels showing the +/-10 dB range of the sliders.

indicators.label: Per-band value label
indicators.meaning: Live dB value of each slider shown below its handle.

related_menu_items: Right-click the 'EQ' applet-tray button > toggle applet visibility
related_user_tasks: Enable radio-side graphic EQ for TX
Enable radio-side graphic EQ for RX
Boost or cut specific octave bands (63 Hz to 8 kHz)
Reset all bands to flat with one click
Switch between shaping RX audio and TX audio
Compare EQ on vs EQ off quickly with the ON button
